reviews.review_text:
NOTE! I encourage you to play The Last of Us Part 1 first, as the events of the first game are a continuation of the adventures

My review will, of course, include comparisons to The Last of Us Part 1! My review of the first game can be found here
https://steamcommunity.com/id/KINGVikingBezimienny/recommended/1888930/

Action adventure game with survival elements. The story takes place several years after the events of the first game, in a world devastated by a fungal epidemic that transforms people into aggressive creatures. The player primarily assumes the role of Ellie, who embarks on a brutal journey motivated by revenge. This is the best adventure game I've ever played! Why? You'll find the answer in the arguments below

✅Once again, I'll describe what I really liked about the first game and what was expanded in the second game, primarily Ellie and Abby's relationships with other characters in The Last of Us Part II, who they were involved with, and how these relationships influence the events of most of the game

✅In my opinion, the plot was more interesting, surprising, and better than in the first game. It was great that we could experience both sides of the story: the ability to play from the perspective of Ellie [spoiler] ,who was seeking revenge (for Joel's death) and at one point had to face the consequences of her decisions, and Abby, who was seeking revenge for her father's death.[/spoiler] Later in the game, quite unexpectedly, both characters finally meet in the same place, leading to a fight. The game had plenty of plot twists for the supporting characters, which I also really liked

✅In terms of graphics, it's once again excellent and better than in the first game. The game has more detailed character models, more realistic facial and movement animations, better lighting, denser foliage, and more refined weather effects and environmental physics. Thanks to this, the characters' facial expressions and behavior more effectively convey their emotions, both in cutscenes and during gameplay

✅The sound is also superior to the first game, although it was already excellent in The Last of Us Part 1. Thanks to the more advanced surround sound, it's easier to pinpoint the origin of enemy and infected sounds. Gunshots, impacts, and explosions sound more natural and realistic. The sound better responds to the environment (echoes, muffled sounds in buildings, creaking buildings, and birdsong).

✅The shooting is well-done because weapons have realistic recoil, and each weapon reacts differently (of course, we can upgrade them for more precise recoil, but that's in the next section). Each shot can affect enemies' reactions through noise, and they know exactly where you fired

✅This part of the game also features an upgrade system. In the workshop, we can upgrade various things, such as larger magazine capacity, faster reloads, better accuracy, or reduced noise (this applies to the silenced pistol). While exploring, we also collect supplements that unlock skill trees like stealth, crafting, health, or weapon upgrades

✅Another advantage, better crafted than in the previous installment, is the more atmospheric and detailed locations, primarily centered around Seattle, which is vast, diverse, and changes with the weather and the story. Compared to the first installment, the locations are more extensive and open, allowing us to search for notes or resources, or find hidden workshops or safes

✅Two distinct character personalities: Ellie, who, after certain events in the game that I won't spoil, is more emotional, impulsive, and often acts on anger, while Abby Anderson is more disciplined, pragmatic, and usually tries to think more rationally

✅We have 6 difficulty levels in the game (Very easy, easy, moderate, hard, survival, brutal). As the difficulty level increases, enemies become more alert and accurate, and resources become more limited

✅New monsters have been added to those from The Last of Us 1 (Runners, Lurkers, Clickers, and Bloaters). "Shamblers" have also been added, which spray clouds of acid gas around them, damaging and blinding them and exploding upon death. A giant boss, the "Rat King," consisting of numerous infected creatures fused together with mycelium, has also been added. New faction, the Rattlesnakes, has emerged, and they are the only human faction to use Clickers and Runners as tools of torture and defense

✅New moves have been added in the second installment, such as dodging and crawling under obstacles, as well as stronger melee attacks (especially for Abby), and improved enemy intelligence

✅The game also contains notes, letters, journals and other documents left by people after the epidemic (I recommend looking for them, we can learn more about what happened during the epidemic, unfortunately I missed some somewhere Xd), as well as collector's cards, coins, training manuals that unlock new branches of skills and safes that may contain resources or upgrades

✅The game's price is appropriate for the game's content

✅After completing the game, you can see the behind-the-scenes of the game's development and other unlockable features

After completing the game, you can see the behind-the-scenes of the game's development and other unlockable features

✅❌How Here everyone always has to decide for themselves whether they like such games because the game is linear in terms of plot and often provides large, semi-open locations to explore, especially in Seattle


❌What I didn't like about the first game, and the second, is that we can't go back to the previous location to explore other areas. Sometimes I'd open a door thinking it led to an optional room, only to find it was a transition to the next stage of the story, with no way to go back and finish exploring

❌Another drawback is that some items, such as gadgets, are unlocked after completing the game, such as additional weapon skins and attachments

✅✅ Verdict: [strike] Terrible [/strike] , [strike] Bad [/strike] , [strike] Mediocre [/strike] , [strike] Decent [/strike], [strike] Good [/strike] , [strike] Very Good [/strike] , [strike] Excellent [/strike]  , [b] Masterpiece [/b]

[quote] If you'd like to see more of my reviews, you can find them on my
curator page:
[url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/45994324/admin/reviews_manage/]CuratorKingViking[/url]
and on my group:
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/groups/CuratorKingViking]GroupKingViking[/url]

If you have any questions about my reviews, feel free to ask.
I’m happy to answer them. All reviews are my own!!
Best regards, King Viking Bezimienny, Polish Game Reviewer. [/quote]

100% Completion

What can I say it's worth every single cent I've spent on it.

Comparing to the first part it has done a big leap forward in terms of gameplay and versatility of how you can proceed through levels. The combat feels more dynamic, stealth is deeper.

I also really enjoyed the Grounded mode. It completely changes the way you approach the game forcing you to think more carefully, manage resources wisely, and adapt your playstyle. It made every encounter feel more intense and rewarding, pushing me to play smarter rather than just relying on common run'n'gun tactics.

Playing 4K has been smooth and immersive.

[h1]This review will contain spoilers for the whole game. [/h1]

TLOU2 is simultaneously the best and worst game I've ever played. I loved The Last of Us 1. I avoided all the leaks pertinent to the second game, and after much anticipation... I was let down beyond words.
My disappointment stems from just how much of a contrast there is between the quality of the narrative and literally everything else within the game. Let me elaborate --
[h2]🎮 Gameplay [/h2]

The gameplay is hands down some of the best (maybe even [i]the[/i] best) I've had the pleasure of engaging with:
[list]
[*]Guns feel great. Although some feel they should pack a lot more of a punch (especially near the end of the game, with the rattlers' helmets being able to withstand a shot from a hunting rifle)
[*]The stealth is amazing. It's so satisfying to outsmart enemies, learn their patrol paths, and execute a plan. Or simply improvise with whatever's at hand. 
[*]Combat as a whole is great. You can take enemies as human shields, prone in foliage to regain stealth or sneak up on enemies, and disable enemies by shooting their legs (which opens them up to lethal strikes or being held as a shield).
[*]Enemy AI is intelligent, but not so intelligent that it's unfun. Enemies will surround you, co-ordinate attacks, and make you have to think outside the box to outsmart them in open combat.
[/list]


[h2]📹 Graphics[/h2]

Honestly, this speaks for itself. The graphics are stunning, and some of the best out there. Beautifully crafted environments, with so much environmental storytelling and attention to detail. Enough said.
[h2]📖 Narrative[/h2]

This is where the game lost me, and is why I simply can't recommend this game to anyone. The story - the heart of the game - is... so terrible. In fact, it's [i]so[/i] bad that the fact that this game continues to have 95% positive reviews is absolutely insane to me. [i]This[/i] won game of the year? Seriously?? Is this our standard for "revolutionary" modern-day storytelling?
I often hear people who love this story say, "you just don't get it". But I get it. Here's what TLOU2's narrative is, summed up in one sentence. Are you ready?

[h3][b]Revenge is bad.[/b][/h3]

Seriously. That's it. The game is 24 hours' worth of "revenge is bad". That's it. That's the story. They butchered the amazing and fan-loved dynamic of Joel and Ellie to tell one of the most [b][u]boring, asinine, run-of-the-mill[/u][/b] stories ever told.

One of the most beloved characters in video game history is killed off within the first two hours of the game by a character we've known for two minutes by comparison - beaten to death by them with a golf club, and then his corpse spat on for good measure. Not to mention, it was [i]sheer coincidence[/i] that Joel [i]happened[/i] to run into Abby 0.1 seconds before she was about to be bitten and save her life. The whole story relies on similar coincidences.

[b][u]The characters in the story are simply set pieces to keep the narrative moving along, nothing more.[/u][/b] What do we know about Dina? Or Jesse? Or Owen? Or any of the new characters that are introduced? We know exactly nothing about them. There's no compelling character arcs for them, they possess no motivations, no goals, they go through zero development and then, when the narrative demands it, they are killed off in spectacular (or unspectacular) ways.

[b][u]Ellie's immunity?[/u][/b] Mentioned once, and never again, overshadowed a [i]pregnancy[/i]. Really??

[b][u]Jesse's backstory?[/u][/b] Dunno. He's just cool, I guess. 'Cos he cares about his friends or something.

[b][u]Dina and Ellie's relationship?[/u][/b] Any relation to the plot or overall story? Nope. It's just... there. Existing. Presumably so things are more emotional for the player when they're beaten almost to death by Abby.

The game [i]makes you kill a dog[/i] to progress and then within half an hour of killing that dog makes it your sidekick for basically the rest of the game because [i]revenge is bad and makes people do bad stuff.[/i]

[i]Half of the game[/i] is a three-day long flashback where you're forced to play as the person who killed Joel. [i]And[/i] this change in perspective happens at what would usually be the climax of any conventional story - but at this point, you're less than halfway through the story. It completely destroys any pacing the game previously had, and is a stupid and jarring time to change perspectives. Not to mention, any potential attachment that you would have formed to Abby's characters is kind of destroyed by the knowledge that these people are already dead.

I seriously cannot stress this enough - the [b][u]WHOLE game[/u][/b] constantly slaps you with "revenge is bad" over and over and over and over again, and the complete and total lack of subtlety in how it explores this generic concept only serves to anger players like myself even further. It makes you kill people to progress, and then makes you interact with those same people in what is essentially a gigantic flashback sequences. It makes you kill dogs, and then makes you play fetch with those same dogs in this flashback sequences. It makes you tear violently through [i]hundreds[/i] of people, killing them in grotesque and chilling ways - stabbing, shooting, exploding, burning them to death - and gives you no choice in this matter, all while telling you that your actions (which, again, the game gives you no agency over) are bad and morally reprehensible. The lack of subtlety in how the game tries so obviously to make you feel bad for choices that aren't even your own makes me roll my eyes and feel preached to about an already obvious concept.

Sometimes, if an enemy is the last in the area and you deliver a non-lethal blow, they will lay down their arms and beg for their life. I tried sparing such enemies numerous times - the game does not allow for it. You [i]must[/i] kill these enemies, or they will get back up after some time and try to kill you. It is [i]so[/i] insulting to be fed this drivel about how "[i]rEvEngE is baAdd!!!"[/i] all whilst you're being given zero choice in the matter. Did I mention they threw away Joel and Ellie's dynamic from the first game for this??

Seriously, it is [i]mind-blowing[/i] that this garbage narrative passed any form of quality assurance or playtesting. How on [i]Earth[/i] this dumpster fire of a narrative was given [i]game of the year[/i] is genuinely just insulting. It's as if Neil Druckmann forgot how to make interesting or compelling characters.

[h1]📖 Narrative - The Good[/h1]

With all this said, the story is still not without merit. The flashback sequences involving Joel and Ellie are amazing. The overall performances of all the actors are phenomenal, and they do sell the story very well, even though the overall narrative is contrived. Despite the plot's weaknesses, I think the strong performances from the cast helped to at least partly sell an otherwise uninteresting and boring story. However in my opinion, the story's weaknesses are vastly more prevalent than its strengths.
This game is life changing. It changed my perspective on all the things I used to take for granted. The impact it had on me is not explainable by words.

La réalisation graphique, les animations et la conception des niveaux comptent parmi les meilleurs sur PS. Les combats d’infiltration sont bien travaillés et l’ambiance post-apocalyptique parfaitement rendue.

Sur le scénario, il s’éloigne des histoires classiques de vengeance pour traiter de la haine et du pardon. Cependant, la mort brutale de Joel au début et le basculement forcé sur Abby divisent énormément les joueurs, beaucoup de fans du premier opus n’adhèrent pas aux personnages ni à la fin.

À essayer si vous appréciez une production de grande qualité ; passez votre chemin si vous tenez aux personnages du premier volet.

So many thoughts and reactions to share on this one, and chances are you have seen them all. This is imo the most polarizing game that has ever been made, people who hate it HATE it and people who love it LOVE it. I have many thoughts on this, to list them briefly: The gameplay is actually quite good, it is intense and visceral whether you play stealthy or guns blazing. On that topic, this is the most violent game I have ever played bar none, genuinely gobsmacked it is not rated Adults Only. Been gaming my entire life and this is the first time violence in a game actually made me a bit uncomfortable. This game made me feel - that sounds silly but it is true. For all its faults, this game brings out emotions. Some good, some bad, some ugly. Now on the topic of ugly, let's address the elephant in the room.

The internet HATES a particular main character, and the discourse around them (and many other things) is UGLY. I don't like them either, but wow, they *aren't even the worst character in this game*. So many of the people in this are genuinely insufferable, and that is by design. This game wants you to hate certain people and then like certain others, only to make you hate them again and it is not shy to use every cheap emotional trick in the book to make what reactions they want you the player to have happen. It just doesn't work. The story has so many issues and plot holes, and while it tries to explore interesting themes (revenge, empathy and perspective) it just doesn't work because the character they want you to go on that journey with, and all of their friends, are AWFUL. Personally I just could not empathize with them when they repeatedly showed just how awful they all are - actions they take constantly had me saying "WOW, these people suck". [spoiler] What could possibly be worse than fully committing to abandoning your pregnant partner and never seeing them again? That's right, cheating on her with your ex the day before you do that and sleeping with said ex in the bed you shared with your pregnant gf! Owen is AWFUL omg [/spoiler] I cheered when one of the side npcs said verbatim to the particular main character "you're a piece of ♥♥♥♥ and you always have been". And I'm sure I'm not alone there. And again, there are so many instances in this game where the people in it do something completely out of pocket and nonsensical, acting stupid or doing things for who knows why, just to advance the misery and make things worse for those in the narrative. This game is Suffering Porn at its absolute worst. I had multiple plot points spoiled but the ones I didn't know I easily guessed - assume the WORST possible outcome for everything, or the one that makes the least sense so everyone will be worse off, and you will predict the entire story of this game. 

I could keep going with many thoughts - some would potentially make me want to recommend, others not so much. I have decided to Not Recommend this game for one simple reason though. I just wish it didn't exist. The first game was so good, the story of it stood alone and that ending was excellent. We didn't need to go on this separate journey with these characters - I would have much preferred this story if it involved an entirely new cast. I don't regret playing this, tbh anything that pisses the internet off this much I at a minimum wanted to see what the fuss was all about. But genuinely, this game didn't need to exist and I kind of wish the first game was where the story ended.

If somehow, the Lord gave me a second chance at this moment, I would not have played this game all over again.

A masterpiece in storytelling exploring the concept of grief and the repercussions of continuing the cycle of violence. The Last of Us II tests the player's ability to overlook bias and emphasize with the "enemy" culminating in a pitiful fight on the beach, if you as the player left that fight wishing for a different outcome then you failed their test. 

From a gameplay perspective, the combat is vastly improved in comparison to the first game and feels very fluid with the addition of dodging and proning. Good selection of weapons for both Ellie and Abby and unique encounters throughout the story, enemy AI is overall coded well which was a feature that I definitely noticed when completing the main story and the roguelike mode, No Return, on grounded difficulty. On the topic of No Return, I would have preferred a revival of Factions multiplayer over it. I enjoyed No Return however since obtaining all achievements related to the mode I have had little desire to play it and find it to be repetitive. 

Graphics wise it's a stunning game if your PC can handle it, had some optimization issues on launch but I imagine those have been patched by now. It was a pleasure to traverse through a post-apocalyptic west coast, amplified by the attention to detail within the environment and OST. Naughty Dog has put extensive detail into the games photo mode and allows for a lot of creative freedom in crafting arguably the best looking screenshots in any video game. 

Thanks for reading, Endure and Survive.

A Top 10 Playstation game and Naughty Dogs best game, but is bogged down with its pacing. 
Thankfully chronological order fixes most of the pacing problems and makes you actual care for Abby/ friends. 
If it was originally launched in chronological order we might have gotten Factions 2.

One of the best story driven games I have ever played. Coming from playing the original on the PS3 to this masterpiece has been such a joy. The Last of Us has such an integral spot in my gaming journey and I will always have a soft spot for this franchise.  10/10
TLOU 1 was a masterpiece--one of my top 3 games ever. This was a slog. I spent half the game wishing it was already over, and by the end I hoped every character would die except Lev.

This series has to be the best storyline I've every played through. It's one of those games that you absolutely have to play sometime in your life. Not to mention that this game has the best combat I've ever seen!

I just finished this game as of about 10 minutes ago. 

I am sobbing. 
The range of emotions that I have felt in the last few minutes, is absolutely insane.  

I remember playing TLOU with my brother YEARS ago when it first was released on PS3, and then to play this game after so long, WITHOUT NEARLY ANY SPOILERS, I am blown away. The story telling, soundtrack, character depth & development, and of course the art, graphics and voice acting. This game was made so well, and it really made me feel for the characters I played as. Without saying much, I found myself in moral dilemmas often.

As for issues I ran into while playing, I did have my game crash twice while playing: the first time was a personal Driver issue with NVIDIA, but the second time seemed to be the game itself struggling to function while having other applications open. Thankfully the game did auto save regularly and so this really did not end up being much of an issue. I also had some weird clipping happen in game, but such is the life of 3D games lol.

I highly recommend this game to anyone who loved the first game, and those who are looking for queer representation in video games. I cannot wait to replay this game, and to rip my heart out of my chest over and over again. 

Thank you, Naughty Dog

its not good. part 1 was great, this one is awful. gameplay wise its still good, but the story and characters are so unbelievably bad that its better to just read a summary rather than waste your time playing the game if you really want to know what happens.
Peak game. Peak gameplay. Peak storytelling. Play it, trust me.

This game may be controversial but for me its a 10/10. The Gameplay is phenomenal and the story is compelling. Im on my third playthrough and the gamemode "no return" adds infinite replay value. Game runs great on my PC and graphics still hold up well even tough it was released on the PS4.

The majority of the game is very fun, with far better gameplay than the first game, but Abby's portion of the game has some of the most infuriating combat encounters from any game i've ever played, I would recommend this game just for no return and the Ellie portion, but with Abby being half the game, and the story decisions I just can't agree with, I simply don't recommend this for people who loved the storybuilding and characters from the first.
loved it. one of the games everyone must play at least once. such a sad/bittersweet ending

This is possibly the BEST story game ive ever played, i dont want to spoil it for new players but the ending of the story was beautifully done and does "show dont tell" in the best way possible. I found lots of people didnt like the "unfortunate" events at the beggining of the game (if you know you know) but i think it was a good idea to move the story along, it makes you want to kill abby and feel connected to ellie. so many other things the developers did with the story i also want to talk about, however i want to keep this review mostly spoiler free so new players can expirence this game for themselves. If you are debating getting this game... i highly recommend you get this game, i would seriously rate this game a 10/10 while last of us 1 a 8/10 (yes the part 2 is that much better in everyway possible)

Fun up until the game forces you to play as Abby for the next 50% of the game. Interesting take on gameplay, but not something I want as a player. No thanks, id rather not. I looked up how it ends, I am gonna save myself the trouble from hating this game even more. I want JUSTICE, not empathy. Would have been great if this were a choice based game that changes the outcome, but its not.
best story telling in a video game between the first and the second and just never lets up. Dont let these p****** who were mad the story didnt go  the way they wanted tell you this is a bad game

Just like Zelda did in the 2000s and Bioshock did in the 2010s, The Last of Us Part 2 managed to do the same in 2020. ~it shows gamers a whole new perspective on what a video game can be.

Unlike other videogames, tlou part 2 it's not a fun game. When you immerse yourself in tlou universe, you will feel depression, sorrow and fury. Technically and visually, it's a masterpiece, but the narrative is what truly breaks you. Since then, game developers haven't been able to make a video game quite like it.

remember when you're lost in the darkness look for the light'')

Easily one of the best story driven games I have ever played. I don't know why people hate this game so much like it's not that deep man just enjoy the game. 10/10 would recommend. [spoiler] i get both the perspective but man... joel didn't deserve all that. [/spoiler]
I've played this about 70 times now on multiple platforms. Best money I have ever spent. This game is the reason I'm now in college for Game Development.

After playing ts for 106 hours i have done 3 playthroughs, one on moderate for the first experience one on very light to collect all of the artifacts and trophys and one more on grounded for that trophy. i am now on my fourth and final playthrough where i will get the final trophy on completing the story in chronilogical order. 100% recommend, may or may not suck the life out of you because of the zombies but if your looking for a good story game to play buy the last of us part 2. the trophys area little bit out of hand so if your a trophy collecter id be cautious. but overall a 9/10. ( took away 1 point because im scared of the zombies😭✌️). 100% RECOMMEND

just a amazing game and a amazing seris

Cant wait for the next one

A natural evolution of The Last of Us in every way, and a testament to the possibilities of story telling in videogames. The Last of Us Part 2 expands on every aspect from the first game and makes it even better, richer, and more depressing. I have played the game twice on PS5, before playing it on the pc.

Found this one even better than the first one, and the first one was great. Almost a masterpiece. Never play this game when you are feeling low, it will make your life even worst. One of the most emotionally devastating game I have played. Every one got their own choice, but I don't see the reason for this much hate for this game
Best graphics in any game of all time. Amazing story. Amazing music just a 100000/10 game overall
Wow just wow, best storytelling i've ever seen video game or movie. The characters are so well done, the graphics are amazing and the gameplay is so good and smooth. 12/10

I was pleasantly surprised to see that Last of Us 2 had alot more to say then just a trite "Revenge is bad" storyline. 
Easily one of the best deliveries of a story in any game ive played. Its damn close to perfect.
I mean what need be said, this just like the first game is a master class in gaming and everyone should try the games at least once.
Best Zombie back story ever! Could live without the WOKE story line. Great game

Still one of the best story mode game on the market rn. gameplay is so satisfying, combat system is diverse, story is very interesting and entertaining but unnecessarily long, graphics are on top level same as the sound design. I would say that part 1 is miles better than 2,  but would still  recommend it 100%.
GREAT story good game play mechincs and a good balance of horror actoin unesyness

Alright I know this game is EXTREMELY controversial, and while I understand some of the criticism, I think a lot of the hate surrounding it is overblown. Part ll is still one of the best games Ive ever played. The story is far from perfect, and there are definitely character decisions that frustrated me as well.

MAJOR SPOILER SECTION That said... The gameplay is an absolute masterpiece. The combat, stealth, movement, animations, level design and attention to detail are incredible. Now for the biggest controversy surrounding the game: Joels death and abbys character Joels death was a huge shock to many fans and I completely understand why. And in my opinion, it happened a little too early and was definitely extremely dark even for a Ip like the last of us. That being said I think his death really makes sense because of the decisions he made in part 1 There was always a feeling that what he did in Salt lake city would come back to haunt him. 

Then theres abby, who is easily the most hated character in the game. While I think she has major flaws and sometimes falls flat. I think she is an a effective antagonist. her motivation makes sense. joel killed her father, and her quest for revenge makes sense also she is not trans as some people literally lied about lol. My major frustration comes from her being an average character at best when she could have been so much better. 

With all the ups and downs of the story, The last of us Part ll is still an incredibly thrilling experience from beginning to end. One thing that I must bring up again is the tone of the game it is night and day from the first game part 1 has plenty of tragedy but part 2 is sooooo much darker to a point it can feel overwhelming and sometimes hopeless but I think the flashbacks with joel really save it from its relentlessly grim story. Despite my criticisms Its still one of the most memorable games ive ever played I Give it a 8 out of 10. Just wanted to add a quick side note the music IS A 10 OUT OF 10 Gustavo Santaolalla is a mad scientist.
Overall, The Last of Us™ Part II Remastered is a masterpiece that delivers an unforgettable gaming experience.
I enjoyed the game overall, but half of the game you play backstory of Abby. There was also a lot of recap episodes. The ending was also kind of a disappointment. But that is how some stories go.
amazing game it is extremly fun and i would do multiple playthroughs

This game is much more brutal, gritty, and realistic than the first, and i love that

It effectively breaks the hero narrative by humanizing characters, making the act of killing feel more impactful. The plot and themes deviate from traditional storytelling, resulting in a sense of realism where characters die without clear reasons or retribution. The focus is on the story rather than fan expectations.

One of the greatest games ever made and easily the most intense emotional experience I've had with a piece of media to date. Keep an open mind, play it for yourself, and make up your own mind. I'd also recommend turning the developer commentary (during cutscenes) on if and when you replay.

The game functioned VERY well for me. Nary a crash or a major bug. There was only a brief glitch wherein a character's hand became all twisted-up but this resolved itself after a few minutes.

I really love how this game works and mechanics. This is one of the best games I've ever played. Best story rich game, it's like a movie like video game which I love the most.  The acting so real which you can become a part of the story.

I will rate perfect 10/10 review and well deserved.

SPOILERS IN REVIEW

From a gameplay perspective, the survival and stealth elements carry over seamlessly, the new weapons are fun to use and the multiple skill trees are a nice addition. 
The story, especially the ending, left me feeling underwhelmed. In my opinion, the best ending would have been to have Abby and Lev ride off into the sunset. Ellie gave up everything for revenge and followed Joel's path, in a lot of ways, which triggered a mental discussion, but I feel it wasn't as substantial as it possibly should've been. 
Overall, I thoroughly enjoyed my time with this game.
absolutely amazing game, the graphics and combat especially great. the story is good, some people just cant handle the fact that ellie is just as bad a person as abby. a true masterpiece.

This game is incredible and a huge step up from the first honestly. I don't understand where the controversy comes from at all. What Naughty Dog does with narrative here - structurally and writing wise - is so astounding and bold. We're given such a beautiful, haunting, and nuanced look at cycles of revenge and grief that rival the best films on the subject, an extremely rare feat for a video game. The level design is so improved over the first game, so much more interesting and diverse to explore and work through. And of course, the presentation is something else, maybe the best I've seen in a game yet. The vision of Seattle is so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ cool and well realized. History will absolve this game for sure.

Whatever preconceptions you might have going into The Last of Us Part II, the noise fades instantly once you actually play it. The game speaks for itself through uncompromising polish across the board.

The narrative and gameplay complement each other fairly. The world has evolved into a much grittier landscape of hardened survivors, and that desperation isn't just a story beat—it is baked right into the fluid, heavy combat loop. The phenomenal voice acting and incredibly detailed audio design ground the entire experience, making the tension of every encounter palpable, especially if you're running a solid headphone setup. Topping off all this, Nixxes again delivered a gold-standard PC port. It is wonderfully optimized right out of the box, letting the native HDR shine and scaling beautifully to take full advantage of high refresh rate and VRR displays.

It is a time capsule in cohesive game design where the visuals, combat, and story all elevate each other, leaving little to be desired or begrudged.

The Last of Us P2 is an incredible game in almost every aspect. The visuals are stunning, the combat feels intense and realistic, the attention to detail in the environments, animations, and sound design is genuinely impressive.

Unfortunately, the optimization holds the experience back. Despite running the game on a strong PC, I constantly run into technical issues that break immersion. Nearly every session ends with me having to either close the game entirely or restart my PC because of freezing, severe frame drops, or instability.

What’s frustrating is that the game can run perfectly fine one moment, then become unstable the next without warning. For a title with this level of polish in its gameplay and presentation, the technical performance feels unfinished. When everything works, this is easily one of the best cinematic action games available on PC. But the optimization issues make it difficult to fully appreciate how great the game truly is.

I’m not leaving a negative review out of frustration, because TLOU2 is genuinely one of the best games I’ve ever played. That’s exactly why the poor optimization is so disappointing. A game with this much quality in its visuals, storytelling, and gameplay deserves technical performance that matches its ambition.
Amazing game, Amazing story, very difficult game. Very emotional. Would recommend to anyone
ending is kinda ass, gameplay is near unbeatable though

Say what you will about the story, this is an exceptional game and possibly the best I have ever played. Outstanding storytelling combined with amazing graphics and combat that somehow improves upon even the first game - it's one of the games that you just have to play if you ever get the chance. The main critiques from most people come in the form of the story. It angered A LOT of people upon its release and still people will hate on this game for it. I, however, think the story takes a profound direction and is a very good example for telling a story that NEEDS to be told rather than the one that people WANT to be told. It is designed deliberately to provoke you. It seems many people missed the point of that, and also what the story's conclusion is supposed to mean for Ellie, but also for you as a player.
super awesome sauce, graphics are crazy good too but in my opinion nothing will beat the first game if youre thinking of buying i reccomend just wait till it goes on sale

I want to say I absolutely loved this game. The music, graphics, gameplay, and story were perfect to me. The combat was a massive improvement on that of the first game (not that it was bad but there was certainly room for improvement). The story, ah the story, was so beautifully written, and as polarizing and depressing as it is, it is beautiful in its own right. It captures every emotion, reaction, moment of hesitation, and regret. It drags you on this rollercoaster of emotions from the death of a certain character in the beginning, to playing as the killer (which I will admit was a bit rocky at first), to the ending. Both characters having to live the realities they created for themselves because they chose revenge over forgiveness and the type of world fostered by those choices, and what a cruel and cold world it is. Regardless of who it could've been filling these shoes, I think this game demonstrated that cycle excellently, and it shows how bleak and empty it is to satisfy these feelings of vengeance. However, as bleak as the ending may seem, both characters live. They live despite the horrible things they've done and are not just given the opportunity but rather the permission to live and not define themselves with the vengeance that ruined their lives and the lives of countless others. I absolutely adore this game, and if you've come this far I apologize, I'm not that great at punctuation, but thank you.
Simply one of the best feel bad games ever made. The game play is so horrifically real it sends chills down your spine.

Incredible game, beautiful graphics, smooth mechanics, and crisp shooting. I know this is an old game but as to not ruin the story for anyone I will just say this game is heart wrenching. Also probably the most stressful game I have ever played, the first one feels like a stroll through the park compared to this one. In summary this is a great game that builds atop probably my greatest game of all time, and I say that not as a huge fan boy but just a fact.

Story spoiler!
[spoiler] The concept of revenge in Part II was a 50/50, i get why Abby did what she did, but the game could've expanded on her more than she did in the game before that moment, like a 40/60 split on which character you're cheering on, but since its out of the blue its more like 5/95, and the 95 goes to Ellie.
If we compare Ellie from part 1 and 2, we can see what the lust for revenge does to someone, Ellie at Jackson compared to her at the end by the beach is already a great comparison, but what she does during the story like torturing people, really shows how much of Joel (even though i don't think he would've wanted to give those traits to her) stuck to her.

Speaking of Joel, a massive thing that happens slowly after Abby meets Lev is that she slowly becomes like Joel, killing anyone for Lev. Abby herself didn't know Joels motivations for killing her father in part I, i think it would've been cool for Ellie to open up or vent at Abby near the end of the game, and for Abby to realise that she's in the same situation that caused all of this.
[/spoiler]

Not the perfect gem the first game is.
The Last of Us part 1 is my favorite story game I've ever played, and is arguably one of the best pieces of media ever released. Interactive, fun, beautiful, and perhaps one of the best told and paced stories ever told. Part 2 is not that. I don't mind the politics of this one but, in just terms of the story this is a horribly paced story. The game is basically too long, and I've found it nearly impossible to not get burnt out about 3/4 of the way through this one. It is simply just too long... I play these games pretty thoroughly and the only way I've found to bear the run time is to just skip a lot of the collectables... one of my favorite parts of part 1. When I initially played this on console I quit after about 30ish hours of playing thoroughly and just couldn't keep on playing at a certain point... there's fake out ending after fake out ending... and this story just really drags in the last portion. This game is nearly twice as long as the first game and taken as two different interweaving stories this almost works but, the final section drags quite a bit. I love the messaging, and I think a story about the depravity of revenge is one worth telling but, it could've been told better.
However...
The gameplay here is in my opinion quite a bit better. The gameplay when in combat is addictive. Part 1 plays a lot like a more stealthy game, almost metal gear solid if you choose to play it like that. The gameplay is entertaining, but somewhat lacks behind the story telling, and after a few playthroughs kind of becomes a bit of a dash to the next cutscene. This game is sort of the opposite, the story telling gets a bit old but, the gameplay here is honestly a blast, certain parts where you are balancing over tall heights do make you feel the fear the characters feel... they are a little too common. However the combat here is just awesome. The enemy AI is some of my favorite I've ever seen, the communication between npcs is unsettling and makes every kill feel like a brutal potentially unnecessary move, and when you want to rampage the pace is so much higher than the first and gives a lot of potential for fun take downs. Rushing around, hopping into the grass for a quick switch to get a stealth kill, utilizing a brick or a bottle in a pinch just feels right. Much of this comes down to the often spectacular level design, there are a few stinkers and more dull fights, but, many of them are just amazing. The enhanced gore system also really elevates the experience, and adds so much oomph to a sometimes lucky feeling headshot or neck shot.
This game looks absolutely amazing, top notch with some cutscenes getting almost scary realistic, this PC port sometimes feels like playing through a live action horror movie, the graphics are slightly more impressive than the PC port of part 1 but, not as insane of an improvement of the PS5 port of this versus the PS4 port of part 1, and that's okay, I like how easy the transition from part 1 to part 2 is in terms of the graphics, the outdoor environments are pretty impressively improved. This game is not easy to run with cranked settings especially at 4k, certain grassy areas that are insanely beautiful do even cause a 4090 to chug and go into the 40s with some chop. Overall the optimization even at launch for this one was better than part 1 from everything I've heard but, with any game going for this level of graphics you will run into the limitations of your hardware.
Overall, Part 2 is worse, much harder to commit to finishing over a weekend, and not even really in my top ten but, I do not hate it. It's a fun game, with some major gameplay improvements to the first game, and I honestly respect the developers for taking on a project so ambitious even if it doesn't land nearly as well as the first. Worth a shot, and it's more The Last of Us that isn't as offensively bad as the show. I'd recommend a try on this one, especially if you crave more in this universe but, it's not quite the sequel I would've liked it to have been.
Whether you love or hate the story, it's worth it just for the graphics, gameplay, mechanics, animations, puzzles, and everything else this game does amazing

The Last of Us Part II is one of the most emotional and controversial games I’ve played. The thing I liked most was Ellie’s character development. Compared to the first game, she feels much more mature, angry, broken, and realistic. You can really feel her emotions through the story, especially how revenge slowly changes her personality. Ashley Johnson’s performance made Ellie feel like a real person instead of just a video game character.

At the same time, I didn’t really like some of the story decisions. Killing Joel at the very beginning felt too sudden and upsetting, especially because many players were attached to him from the first game. I understand the game wanted to shock players and show the cycle of revenge, but sometimes the plot direction felt strange and emotionally exhausting.

Even with those problems, the gameplay itself is amazing. The graphics and details are incredible, and the gore and damage system looks very realistic, which makes combat feel intense and brutal. The fighting system is also improved a lot compared to the first game. There are more stealth options, smoother movement, better enemy AI, and more ways to approach battles. Every fight feels tense and cinematic.

Overall, The Last of Us Part II is a beautiful but divisive game. I may not agree with all the story choices, but the character acting, atmosphere, graphics, and gameplay improvements still make it one of the most memorable games I’ve played.

Not as good as the first game, but how could you top THAT? Very emotionally painful, but still such an interesting story. Wish I'd found a way not to kill the german shepherds :(

Having progressed a little further, I have a few additional thoughts. Dina is an interesting element, I just want to see MORE of her. Why were her and Ellie friends to begin with? Who is Dina really? Who is Jesse? Connection was the core of the first game. But this game, it feels more... isolated. You play alone for so long, that it's become harder for more to play for more than an hour at a time. 

That being said, there are REALLY good parts of this game. The flashbacks are absolute PEAK. The radio station part had me pausing the game A LOT, I was so scared. The music in that part as well? My god. The cycle of revenge is a very interesting angle for the story to go. All in all, it is a good game, but not as strong a title as part 1. When the game is good, it is really good. When it is not, it's kinda just, fine.
story questionable at times but fire game nonetheless, combat peak.

It was great! Waited 6 years to play this now that it is finally here on Steam. Had only minor hesitation just because I REMEMBER the gaming community being in an uproar about characters being LGBTQ, particularly the "T" component. I wondered if the game sacrificed any of its founding elements and traditional storytelling or gameplay in the name of being relevant alongside modern social topics, and to me that would have been a turnoff, but that was not the case even the slightest.

The Last Of Us 2 continues the tale of what is ultimately a world drowned in savagery. Savagery both in the name of meaning, as well as for the sole sake of being savage. There are new factions, groups, and friendly faces which enlighten the characteristics of those doomed to live in this world with no hope of getting better. And still, these people struggle with severe emotional distress, sexuality, friendship, community... They learn they are still only human. I did not find any of the characters' or their identities to be out of place in this continuation of the story of the Last of Us. In fact, I found I learned so much more about people for who they are as well as the hardships they endure, and even in a world completely falling apart, hatred still spreads like the infection itself. And every character was humanized and a connection grown towards, ultimately revealing the soft core of vulnerability inside of all living mortal things. We learn that there were never bad people in the plagued US of A, just people trying to survive, or even people fighting for a reason why they should bother to stay alive.

Additionally, the gameplay is much better in this title compared to the original, with newer gameplay mechanics, weapons, and craft-able items. All in all, if you were attached to the original, your feelings will be hurt, but the story makes a greater point all in all: this is no fairy tale, people can be heartless.

People say this game's story is not that good compared to the first one, which comes to the taste of whoever is playing, but nobody can deny that what this game may lack on story, it exceeds in gameplay. Either way this is a 10/10 game that i would recommend to anybody interested on the genre + it has a roguelike which, if you're bored of playing the main game, you can try this mode which is only about fighting. so if you're here only for the gamplay aspect of the game, you can completly ignore the main story and just play this mode instead. again, 10/10 game.

firstly the story, 
this game is a lot. there are a lot of controversial choices the devs made with this game. they made those creative decisions because they had a vision for the story that isn't comfortable. its commendable that they stood there ground on such a hard story. it confronts many emotional topics all wrapped up in a brutal apocalyptic scenario. this wasn't my first playthu of this game. initially, I got this on release and while I didn't hate the game it wasn't a masterpiece for me at the time. as years have gone by since then the game as aged wonderfully. 

Gameplay/ Visuals 
its snappy and all the animations are buttery smooth. the physics of violence in this game is simply at another level. something i remember from my first playthu was cringing at a blood splatter on a window from a dudes head i had just exploded with a silenced pistol shot. the gore is visceral and if you have a PC that can handle this game at high graphic levels it can be both haunting and beautiful. performance wise this is now pretty optimized. i don't have a high end pc it was mid tier about 5 years ago. i can run the game on medium settings with minimal slow down or visual bugs because my rig is struggling to handle the work load. 

one side note, i think this is the best version of this story. while the HBO adaption has a wonderful first season that is a 11/10 for me the second season falters. its not awful it just doesn't have the same weight that the game does and i attribute that to it being a medium you watch rather then actively engaging in with a story that makes you feel complicit in doing terrible things in the pursuit of justice at any cost. along with some writing changes that really don't land. so play the game instead. 

in total this game is a masterpiece both in the concepts/themes it confronts as well as graphicly.

The Last of Us Part II builds on the world and characters from the first game and takes them somewhere unexpected. It plays with one of storytelling's most compelling ideas: that there are few real villains, just people shaped by loss and hardship, each convinced they're the one doing the right thing.

What stuck with me isn't just how believable the world feels, but the story's willingness to make you sit with characters you'd rather not sympathize with. It earns its emotional moments through that, and through some of the most genuine character writing I've come across in a game.

If you care about story and character, it's hard to think of a better example of what games can do.
hELLLLLLL yeahhhhh. This game is nice. Fighting Rat King with max difficulty is one true game play

The story and relationships presented in the game are undeniably polarising, I'm not going to beat around the bush. If you already know what happens and you are not okay with it, [b]ignore my recommendation and stick to Part I.[/b] If you are okay with it, [i]double check.[/i] 

If you are keen on playing despite everything, I'd recommend playing on a TV using a controller. I played the game through Steam Link and had a grand time. But the beauty of PC gaming is options, play how you want!

[u]Just get through the prologue within the first two hours so you can refund the game if you need to,[/u]

The combat and controls are a major improvement over the first game. Everything flows so much better. On top of that, I really did love the story despite a lot of people disagreeing. I don't think it dragged on at all, I thought it was perfectly paced.

So.... TLOU3 when??

SPOILERS!
Short version:
The game is pure sh!t for the most part. Would not recommend it to anyone- and would not even mention it in the same room as anyone who is an OG fan.

Long version:
- Gameplay 8/10: It's actually good gameplay- the only thing I would say is that it's very repetetive (same enemies over and over). The fighting style is up to the player, wether you want to play sneaky or just shoot anyone you see, which is nice. The crafting- and weapon system is nicely built, almost exactly as in the first game. I also like that you get different weapons depending on if you play Ellie or Abby.

- Story 0/10: What can I say? It's pure azz. It didn't really feel like a sequel to the first game. This game feels like an idea that the creator got and wanted to do- so he had to add several things to the first game that didn't really exist or was even mentioned back then just so that this game would make some sense. I can also see what they were trying to do with Ellie and Abby; "Different sides of the same coin" but they really messed that whole thing up. Since we barely got any time with Abby and her dad I didn't feel a connection like I did with Ellie and Joel, so while I understand why Abby killed Joel, I didn't really feel any sympathy for her or her dad. I also didn't like how they split the story up, so that in the beginning you're with Ellie and then later on with Abby. Because I'm gonna be honest- idgaf about Abby nor Lev, so following their story felt like a never ending story. It sucked. I could go on and on about how much I hate the storyline but I feel like people either love this game or hate it. People who hate it can probably see where I'm coming from so I'm gonna leave it at here. 

- Characters 3/10: The only characters I truly liked was Joel, Ellie and Tommy. Abby according to me was utterly unlikeable, she was annoying in my opinion- same goes for every single one of the side characters in her story. Her and Owen's relationship made me want to end myself, it was too messy, unrelated and not to mention he had a pregnant girlfriend at the same time. Her and her dad's relationship is trash, we saw him in 4 scenes- tops. So I didn't really get Abby's actions in the game because we can barely see them interact, which is also a reason as to why she's so unlikeable. (Because most of her actions is related to her father's Death). Joel and Ellie's relationship was complex but in a good way, we can see both the good parts and bad which makes the relationship feel more real and relatable. And since we know their background it also makes more sense why their relationship has turned out the way it did. Their relationship feels genuine. Same goes for Tommy and Joel, we know that they've had some troubles but you can still see that they deeply care for one another which also makes it feel more genuine and real. Ellie's side characters, Dina and Jesse was okay. Didn't really have any strong feelings about them, Jesse was a good friend and Dina was a food girlfriend, but I didn't really get any Deep conncetion with either of them.

- Ending 0/10: I am so tired of games having this trope. "Revenge isn't the answer". Because sometimes it is, I feel like when someone's wronged you, you don't owe them anything, but sometimes you owe yourself something. And yes revenge is a cycle but so is everything, cycle of Life, the climate cycle and so on. And I believe that even if a cycle ends "badly" it doesn't mean that it ends wrong. If I was Abby- then yes I would also like to end Joel- but at the same time if I was Ellie I would like to end Abby. So even if I bawled my Eyes out when Joel died and it was a bad ending for him, it doesn't mean that his story ended wrong. I just wish that they wrote his Death differently and we got some more time with him. And in Ellie's case I feel like it would just have been better if she killed Abby when she had the chance. And for those saying that then Lev would go after Ellie and the cycle would continue- just end him too. I didn't like lil bro and he was literally unconsious in that scene. That might sound heartless but I stand by karma and revenge so in the end even with both of them dead- Ellie would have had a bad ending too, she ends up alone- which is her worst fear. I have a lot more to say about this whole game and how badly it was written- but I feel like no one will read this anyways, so I'm going to stop here. 

Just don't play the game.
Don't agree with the unnecessary story elements that seem forced into the game. Just make a good sequel. Game looks good on PC at least.
